relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The mission arrives at Thessalonica’s synagogue.
Paul reasons from Scripture over three Sabbaths.
He opens Messiah suffering and resurrection around Jesus.
Some Judeans, many reverent Greeks, and leading women join.
Jealousy gathers the marketplace into uproar.
The accusation says the world-upturners have arrived.
Another king, Jesus, is heard as imperial threat.
City and crowd are troubled.
Security is taken and release follows.
Night sends Paul and Silas to Berea.
Berea receives eagerly and examines Scripture daily.
Many trust, including honorable Greek women and men.
Opposition follows the word to Berea.
Paul is sent toward the sea while Silas and Timothy remain.
Paul reaches Athens and calls his companions.
Athens’ idols provoke Paul’s spirit.
He reasons in synagogue and marketplace.
Philosophers hear Jesus and resurrection as foreign divinity speech.
The Areopagus asks to hear the new teaching.
Strange things in the ears invite explanation.
Athens is hungry for newer speech.
Paul begins by honoring their reverence.
The unknown altar becomes doorway to proclamation.
The Maker of world and all things is not contained in handmade temples.
The Divine needs no service as lack, but gives life and breath to all.
Human nations share one origin and ordered dwelling.
Seeking and finding are possible because the Divine is near.
In the Divine we live, move, and are; we are offspring.
Divine offspring should not reduce the Divine to crafted matter.
Ignorance is now met by the call to turn.
Justice is set through the raised appointed human.
Resurrection divides mockery from further listening.
Paul leaves the Areopagus.
Some join and trust, including Dionysius and Damaris.
